claim: All H1B visas are being cancelled immediately
explanation: No such order exists. H1B visas currently in effect remain valid. This rumor originated from a misrepresentation of a border security executive order that only applies to new visa applications at the border, not existing holders.
viralHeadline: BREAKING: Trump to cancel all H1B visas by April

claim: F1 students will be deported if they participate in campus protests
explanation: While visa holders can face consequences for certain activities, peaceful participation in lawful protests is protected. ICE has not issued any blanket policy targeting student protesters. Individual cases may vary — consult your DSO or an attorney if concerned.
viralHeadline: ICE arresting F1 students at campus protests nationwide

claim: OPT is being eliminated for STEM graduates
explanation: STEM OPT extensions remain fully in place. USCIS has only announced longer processing times, not elimination. Students should apply earlier than usual — at least 90 days before OPT expiry.
viralHeadline: STEM OPT being abolished — thousands of students at risk

claim: H1B holders cannot travel outside the US and re-enter
explanation: H1B holders can generally travel and re-enter with a valid visa stamp and approved petition. However, DHS has issued an advisory recommending caution during the current policy review period. Travel is not banned — but consulting an attorney before international travel is currently advisable.
viralHeadline: H1B workers stranded abroad — banned from re-entering US

claim: Nepali immigrants are being specifically targeted by new immigration rules
explanation: There are no immigration policies specifically targeting Nepali nationals. Current policies apply broadly across nationalities. This claim appears to have originated from social media without any verified government source.
viralHeadline: Nepal added to immigration watch list — visas under review
